title: Hi-Way 40 Update
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 18, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Elaine Wiseman, Housing Administrator
Monica Kirch, Director, Housing and Neighborhood Development
DEPT: Housing and Neighborhood Development
SUBJECT: Hi-Way 40 Update

In March 2022, the City entered into an agreement with Volunteers of America (VOA) to provide $2,000,000 in Coronavirus State and Local Fiscal Recovery Funds (SLFRF) for the acquisition of the Hi-Way 40 Motel. An additional $350,000 in SLFRF funds was allocated in July 2023 for the acquisition of the Hi-Way 40 Motel. The goal of the Hi-Way 40 project is to substantially rehabilitate the dilapidated Hi-Way 40 Motel into 28 units of Permanent Supportive Housing (PSH) for individuals experiencing homelessness, with incomes at or below 30% Area Median Income (AMI), who also have a mental or physical disability.

Escrow closed on the Hi-Way 40 Motel property in September 2023. Following acquisition, the Hi-Way 40 project entered pre-construction activities, including architectural design, interior demolition, and environmental remediation. During the pre-construction phase, significant asbestos-containing materials were discovered, requiring extensive abatement beyond initial projections.

Construction formally began in May 2025. Due to the age and deteriorated condition of the existing Hi-Way 40 Motel structures, the Hi-Way 40 project has experienced continued unforeseen rehabilitation challenges. These conditions have resulted in higher than anticipated construction costs which had resulted in a funding gap necessary to bring the Hi-Way 40 project to completion.

With the City's support, VOA applied for funding through the Nevada Attainable Housing Account (NAHA) in December 2025 in the amount of $1,479,476 to address the remaining funding shortfall. On February 5, 2026, the NAHA Council subsequently awarded the Hi-Way 40 project the full requested amount of $1,479,476, providing the required funding to bring the Hi-Way 40 project to completion.

Construction is anticipated to be completed by the end of March 2026, with lease-up expected to begin in April 2026. Upon completion and occupancy, the Hi-Way 40 project will provide 28 units of permanent supportive housing serving the community's most vulnerable residents.

As the Hi-Way 40 project nears completion, Housing and Neighborhood Development staff will keep Mayor and City Council apprised of grand opening events and opportunities to recognize the Hi-Way 40 project completion as an important milestone for the community.

fields.authors: Elaine Wiseman, Housing Administrator; Monica Kirch, Director, Housing and Neighborhood Development
fields.tldr: The City partnered with Volunteers of America (VOA) to rehabilitate the Hi-Way 40 Motel into 28 units of Permanent Supportive Housing for homeless individuals with disabilities earning at or below 30% AMI. The project faced unforeseen cost overruns due to extensive asbestos abatement and deteriorated building conditions. A $1,479,476 grant from the Nevada Attainable Housing Account (NAHA), awarded February 5, 2026, closed the remaining funding gap. Construction is expected to finish by end of March 2026, with lease-up beginning in April 2026.
fields.key_stats: 28 units of Permanent Supportive Housing; total known public funding: $2,000,000 (initial SLFRF) + $350,000 (additional SLFRF) + $1,479,476 (NAHA) = $3,829,476; target population: individuals at or below 30% AMI with mental or physical disability experiencing homelessness
fields.key_decisions: NAHA Council awarded Hi-Way 40 the full requested amount of $1,479,476 on February 5, 2026, resolving the project funding gap and enabling completion.
fields.action_items: Housing and Neighborhood Development staff will keep Mayor and City Council informed of grand opening events and opportunities to recognize the Hi-Way 40 project completion as a community milestone.

projects: Hi-Way 40 Motel Rehabilitation / Permanent Supportive Housing Project
Coronavirus State and Local Fiscal Recovery Funds (SLFRF)
dollar_amounts: $2,000,000 – initial SLFRF allocation for Hi-Way 40 acquisition (March 2022)
$350,000 – additional SLFRF allocation for Hi-Way 40 acquisition (July 2023)
$1,479,476 – NAHA award to address funding shortfall (February 5, 2026)
dates: March 2022 – City entered agreement with VOA; initial $2,000,000 SLFRF allocation
September 2023 – Escrow closed on Hi-Way 40 Motel property
December 2025 – VOA applied for NAHA funding
February 5, 2026 – NAHA Council awarded $1,479,476 to Hi-Way 40
End of March 2026 – Anticipated construction completion
people: Monica Kirch, Director, Housing and Neighborhood Development
